title: orchis morio subsp. longicornu
intro: Orchis morio subsp. longicornu, commonly known as the green orchid or long-horned orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to Europe and North Africa. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant and diuretic properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues and promote urination. The health benefits of Orchis morio subsp. longicornu include its ability to help alleviate bronchitis, coughs, and other respiratory problems, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to note that its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to the risk of side effects and interactions with other medications.
